mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate positive from negative return distributions with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand recovery, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC is negative over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during a post-recession expansion phase reflect strengthening corporate output capacity and earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power when filtered to recovery regimes only.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the threshold signal shows no statistical separation from baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting higher corporate revenue expectations and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect Rank IC drops below zero in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth during a monetary easing cycle confirms that fiscal and monetary stimulus successfully translates into aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 readings during easing cycles are followed by negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the regime-filtered signal shows no predictive alpha.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0% signals severe supply-side contraction and imminent earnings downgrades, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of observations following a >1.0% INDPRO drop, or the threshold signal fails to reject the null of zero predictive power.
